identity.title: Workflow Cannon Backend Sync Evolution
identity.summary: Three-phase evolution from Git-hot-path task sync to local-first outbox, service-backed dashboard reads, and pluggable canonical backends.

goals: Phase 1: local-first mutations with outbox and background Git sync
Phase 2: local runtime service owns dashboard reads and sync worker
Phase 3: pluggable CanonicalStateSyncBackend with Git as one adapter
Dashboard and agents read local state without Git on the hot path
Multi-agent parallel work with explicit sync posture and conflict handling
nonGoals: Replace Git event-log branch format in Phase 1
Fully implement hosted/Postgres backends in Phase 3
Commit SQLite projections to feature branches

valueAssessment.impact: High — removes manual sync chores, speeds dashboard, unlocks team/multi-agent backends
valueAssessment.rationale: BACKEND.md maps to existing task-state-git machinery with incremental phases

riskAssessment.description: Local projection diverges from canonical Git head
riskAssessment.mitigation: Outbox status, conflict rows, closeout drain gates

riskAssessment.description: Background sync hides failures from operators
riskAssessment.mitigation: Dashboard sync badge, doctor warnings, failed/conflict counts

riskAssessment.description: Agents bypass service/outbox and use legacy Git paths
riskAssessment.mitigation: Update .ai runbooks; doctor detects legacy paths

technicalImpact.compatibilityNotes: Git workflow-cannon/task-state branch remains compatible; tasks.canonicalAuthority git-event-log maps forward
technicalImpact.migrationImpact: Phased rollout: outbox first, then service opt-in, then backend abstraction

architecture.overview: Local mutation → SQLite projection + outbox → background sync adapter → optional Git/hosted backend; dashboard reads service snapshots.

decisions.decision: Keep Git event log as Phase 1 backend
decisions.rationale: Preserves audit trail while demoting Git from hot path

decisions.decision: Introduce local runtime service in Phase 2
decisions.rationale: Avoid repeated wk child processes for dashboard reads
Git becomes one adapter, not the architecture

diagrams.title: Target runtime flow
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A[Agent/CLI/Dashboard] --> B[Local runtime service]
  B --> C[SQLite projection + outbox]
  C --> D[Background sync adapter]
  D --> E[Git backend]
  D --> F[Future hosted backend]

uiUxDirection.summary: Dashboard sync badge and data-source mode; service-backed slice refresh

criticalPaths: Outbox batch publish and conflict handling

implementationGuidance: Follow maintainer delivery loop: branch from release/phase-N, run-transition start/complete
Do not hand-edit workspace-kit.db or task-state-events.jsonl
Source spec: BACKEND.md at repo root
whatNotToDo: Do not remove task-state-publish — it stays admin/recovery
Do not commit SQLite blobs to feature branches
Do not block mutations on Git publication in Phase 1
Do not bake Git terminology into generic backend types
assumptions: Phases 119–121 will be registered in kit_phase_catalog before finalize-to-phase
Current git-event-log authority remains default through Phase 1
HostedApiBackend full implementation stays out of scope until design (T-BE-205) is approved

wbs.title: Inventory existing task-state sync paths
goalMapping: Phase 1: local-first mutations with outbox and background Git sync
wbs.approach: Trace task-state-publish, hydrate, status, snapshot, and mutation publish paths; document sync vs deferred publish and expectedHeadSha usage.
acceptanceCriteria: Implementation note maps every mutation path to publish behavior
Identifies commands that block on Git publication
Lists test files protecting existing behavior
wbs.doneMeans: T-BE-001 complete — sync path inventory published; operators can verify via CLI or dashboard evidence

wbs.title: Add canonical event outbox schema
goalMapping: Phase 1: local-first mutations with outbox and background Git sync
wbs.approach: Add kit_canonical_event_outbox SQLite table with idempotent migration and status index.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration is idempotent
Outbox absent-safe for older workspaces
Tests cover migration and basic CRUD
wbs.doneMeans: T-BE-002 complete — outbox schema migrated; operators can verify via CLI or dashboard evidence

wbs.title: Add outbox repository/runtime API
goalMapping: Phase 1: local-first mutations with outbox and background Git sync
wbs.approach: Implement enqueue, list pending, mark publishing/published/failed/conflict, stale reset, and status repository API.
acceptanceCriteria: Events idempotent by event_id
Pending events returned in deterministic order
Stale publishing rows reset safely
testingVerification: Unit tests for CRUD and status
wbs.doneMeans: T-BE-003 complete — outbox API stable; operators can verify via CLI or dashboard evidence

wbs.title: Queue events locally from mutation commands
goalMapping: Phase 1: local-first mutations with outbox and background Git sync
Dashboard and agents read local state without Git on the hot path
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Queue events locally from mutation commands
wbs.approach: Mutations write local SQLite and enqueue canonical events instead of blocking on Git publish; preserve versions and policy gates.
acceptanceCriteria: Mutating commands update local SQLite immediately
Canonical events queued in outbox
Tests prove mutation works when Git unavailable
wbs.doneMeans: T-BE-004 complete — local-first mutations; operators can verify via CLI or dashboard evidence

wbs.title: Add background outbox publisher
goalMapping: Phase 1: local-first mutations with outbox and background Git sync
Multi-agent parallel work with explicit sync posture and conflict handling
wbs.approach: Background batch publisher with retry/backoff, conflict detection, and configurable canonicalPublishQueue settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Pending rows publish in batches
Conflicts do not spin forever
Publisher can be disabled
wbs.doneMeans: T-BE-005 complete — background publisher shipping; operators can verify via CLI or dashboard evidence

wbs.title: Update runbooks to hide routine sync mechanics
goalMapping: Phase 1: local-first mutations with outbox and background Git sync

goalMapping: Phase 2: local runtime service owns dashboard reads and sync worker
Dashboard and agents read local state without Git on the hot path

wbs.title: Move background sync worker into service
goalMapping: Phase 2: local runtime service owns dashboard reads and sync worker
Multi-agent parallel work with explicit sync posture and conflict handling
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Move background sync worker into service
wbs.approach: Service owns outbox polling, batch publish, hydrate schedule, conflict detection, and flush on demand.
acceptanceCriteria: Sync worker starts with service
Worker can pause/resume and flush on demand
CLI still works without service as fallback
wbs.doneMeans: T-BE-105 complete — sync centralized in service; operators can verify via CLI or dashboard evidence

goalMapping: Phase 2: local runtime service owns dashboard reads and sync worker

wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Add event stream for dashboard and agents
wbs.approach: SSE or local IPC stream for dashboard.slice.updated, task-sync.status.changed, and reconnect after restart.

Event stream reconnects after service restart

phaseRecommendations.label: Phase 119
phaseRecommendations.rationale: Phase 1 — Local-first outbox + background Git sync (T-BE-001–007)

phaseRecommendations.label: Phase 120
phaseRecommendations.rationale: Phase 2 — Local runtime service for dashboard and sync (T-BE-101–107)
